source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The magnitude and phase of the complex Fourier series coefficients \( a_k \) of a periodic signal \( x(t) \) are shown in the figure. Choose the correct statement from the four choices given. Notation: \( \mathbb{C} \) is the set of complex numbers, \( \mathbb{R} \) is the set of purely real numbers, and \( \mathbb{P} \) is the set of purely imaginary numbers.

choice_map.D: The information given is not sufficient to draw any conclusion about \( x(t) \).

extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image object representation, not a path/URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter A to choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the magnitude and phase coefficients are 'shown in the figure'
the needed evidence appears to be the visual pattern of Fourier-series coefficient magnitude/phase, not fully recoverable from text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows the image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the task requires interpreting plotted Fourier-series coefficient magnitude and phase, then mapping that pattern to a property of x(t)
there are 4 semantic choices (real / purely imaginary / complex non-real / insufficient information), implying classification after visual interpretation
the problem likely needs combining more than one cue from the figure (at least coefficient symmetry/phase structure plus signal-type rule), but exact visual complexity is not visible here
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests some multi-step or alternative-reasoning potential, though this is only weak supporting evidence
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the answer_text matches one of the provided choices exactly
the problem is effectively multiple-choice with a closed set of 4 options, making final-answer verification straightforward
image asset exists, so a downstream checker could verify whether the plotted coefficient symmetry supports the selected option
